calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-17-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Natalie Jewelry, Inc., dba Golden Opportunities, a corporation, and Natalie Ladin, an individual.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Marc T. Cefalu, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Bank of the West.
Tyerone Ty, Custodian of Records for plaintiff Bank of the West, was placed under oath and testified.
Mr. Ty appeared via CourtCall for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendants Natalie Jewelry, Inc., dba Golden Opportunities, a corporation, or Natalie Ladin, an individual.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract and Personal Guarantee was filed November 7, 2014, and personally served on defendant Natalie Jewelry, Inc., dba Golden Opportunities' Authorized Agent for Process on July 22, 2015.
Default as to defendant Natalie Jewelry, Inc., dba Golden Opportunities, a corporation, was entered on November 23, 2015.
Plaintiff's Summons & Complaint were personally served on defendant Natalie Ladin, an individual, on September 28, 2017.
Order DENYING defendant Natalie Landin's Motion to Dismiss was filed February 15, 2018.
Default as to defendant Natalie Ladin, an individual, was entered on April 30, 2018.
DOE defendants were dismissed on March 4, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Bank of the West, and against the defaulted defendants Natalie Jewelry, Inc., dba Golden Opportunities, a corporation, and Natalie Ladin, an individual, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $54,625.75.
Attorney Fees - $2,000.00.
Costs - $1,953.72.

Total - $58,579.47.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Marc Cefalu, Esq. 415-438-4600 of Cox Wootton Lerner Griffin & Hansen LLP for Plaintiff.
Natlie Ladin 954-226-8290 Defendant in Pro Per through Court Call.
Spanish Interpreter Ellen Rosenthal is present.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues. The Court identifies issues for the parties. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NATALIE LADIN's Motion To Dismiss.

Defendant Natalie Ladin's motion to dismiss is denied. The court has treated this motion as seeking the discretionary dismissal of the complaint due to plaintiff Bank of the West's failure to serve the summons and complaint on Ms. Ladin within two years of filing this case per CCP 583.420(a)(1). Because the Bank has shown a credible excuse for the delay and had acted with at least modest diligence, the court exercises it discretion not to dismiss this action. Denying the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Jan-16-2018 Continued To Feb-15-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Natalie Ladin, T:954.226.8290, defendant/moving party appearing on her own behalf, with Stephanie De Latour, T:917.995.8023, Spanish interpreter, both by Court Call;
Marc T. Cefalu, Esq. of Cox Wootton Lerner Griffin & Hansen LLP, T:415.438.4600, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, DEFENDANT NATALIE LADIN'S MOTION TO DISMISS is continued to February 15, 2018 at 9:30 am. The Court orders Ms. Ladin to file a declaration stating that she has never been incarcerated and that she has been in the same residence from the period 2014 until the present and did not try to evade service. The declaration must be filed no later than January 31, 2018. Plaintiff may file an opposition no later than February 9, 2018. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
